timeline.notes: Opponent reached both hands to the head + Level change + Shot Double leg from the outside.  Secured Double leg takedown in side control by pinning opponents hips to the opposite side.
Von Flue Choke attempt, using shoulder pressure to the opponent's neck when they hold on to the guillotine while you are in Side Control.
Good job trapping the arms to the side with your chest.  Take this opportunity to gift wrap and move to higher mount position to submit or move the back to submit.

When opponents back is flat on the mat use the arm under his head to apply shoulder pressure to his face to turn his head away from you.  If your opponent cant turn his head towards you then he wont be able to face you to start his escape.

Did not apply head pressure while trying to maintain North/South position.  Recommendation would be to get over under grips in North/South lifting the shoulder with the overhook grip and apply head pressure to the chest sternum area.

Did not apply head pressure while trying to maintain North/South position.  Recommendation would be to get over under grips in North/South lifting the shoulder with the overhook grip and apply head pressure to the chest sternum area.
Missed opportunity to Reverse Kesa Gatame pass.

drills: Torreando to knee slice chain drill
Leg drag to side control drill
Body lock pass to mount flow
Kimura grip to sweep or submit flow
Gift wrap from mount to back take
Heavy top ride: partner tries to escape (3 min)
Knee on belly pressure and transition drill

analysis.ai_summary:
# 🤖 AI SUMMARY REPORT

**Match Outcome:** Won by points - Strong performance with room for optimization
**Final Score:** A: 6 | B: 0

## 🔍 Key Performance Insights
**Dominant Areas:** Composure / IQ, Overall Game Plan
**Critical Gaps:** Guard Passing, Submissions (Offense), Mount Offense
**Scoring Moments:** 2 successful scoring actions
**Missed Opportunities:** 6 identified chances for improvement

## 🎯 Key Missed Opportunities
**Could have maintained position** (occurred 3 times)
**Could have finished submission** (occurred 1 time)
   • Drill submission setups with more precision
   • Practice maintaining control during finish attempts
**Could have improved position** (occurred 1 time)
   • Work on position advancement chains
   • Study transition timing from timeline moments

**1. Guard Passing** (Priority: HIGH)
   • Headquarters position maintenance drill (10 min daily)
   • Pressure passing vs speed passing situation awareness
   • Study guard player's retention patterns from timeline

**2. Submissions (Offense)** (Priority: HIGH)
   • Submission chain flow: primary > backup > position
   • Setup drilling without resistance for timing
   • Study missed opportunities from timeline events

**3. Mount Offense** (Priority: HIGH)
   • Focus on drilling fundamentals with resistance
   • Positional sparring from disadvantageous positions
   • Video study of high-level athletes in this area

## 📊 Tactical Pattern Analysis

## 🎮 Next Match Game Plan
**Primary Strategy:** Focus on proven strengths while shoring up critical gaps
**Lean Into:** Composure / IQ - Your most reliable weapon
**Avoid Early:** Guard Passing - Work this in training first

**Pre-Match Preparation:**
   • Drill top 2 priority areas for 15 minutes before competing
   • Visualization: Successfully executing your strongest techniques
   • Game plan: Lead with strengths, use improved areas situationally

## 📅 Recommended Training Split
**Intensive Phase:** 70% fundamentals, 30% advanced techniques
**Weekly Focus:** 3x positional drilling, 2x live rolling, 1x specific techniques
